mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion, which typically translates into increased consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 55% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, indicating an impending or ongoing economic slowdown that will negatively impact corporate profits and lead to lower S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is above a critical threshold and inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, is accelerating, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the combined signal and SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent growth in retail sales during an established economic expansion indicates robust consumer demand and confidence. This directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances during economic expansions, or the average 5d return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in industrial production after a period of contraction signals a recovery in manufacturing and supply chains. This indicates improved corporate output and capacity utilization, fostering positive long-term equity market sentiment and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low Federal Funds Rate environment, characteristic of accommodative monetary policy, reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment. This supports higher equity valuations and positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in non-farm payrolls and housing starts indicates a robust labor market and high consumer confidence. This drives increased demand for housing and related sectors, boosting overall corporate earnings and S&P 500 performance over the medium term.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or the combined signal's Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarter-over-quarter annualized GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy. This leads to increased corporate revenues and profits across various sectors, which typically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 20d return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
